story.soThat: minimal image size ile deploy edebilleyim
story.tasks:
Task 1: Create multi-stage Dockerfile (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4) - Subtask 1.1: Stage 1 - Dependencies stage (npm ci --only=production) - Subtask 1.2: Stage 2 - Build stage (npm run build, npx prisma generate) - Subtask 1.3: Stage 3 - Production stage (node:20-alpine base, copy artifacts, non-root user) - Subtask 1.4: Configure EXPOSE 3000 and CMD ["node", "dist/main"]
Task 2: Optimize image size (AC: #5) - Subtask 2.1: Verify Alpine Linux base image - Subtask 2.2: Exclude dev dependencies from production stage - Subtask 2.3: Test build and measure image size (<200MB target) - Subtask 2.4: Optimize layers if needed (order, caching)
Task 3: Add health check (AC: #6) - Subtask 3.1: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction to Dockerfile - Subtask 3.2: Configure health check: curl -f http://localhost:3000/health - Subtask 3.3: Set interval: 30s, timeout: 10s, retries: 3 - Subtask 3.4: Test health check in running container
Task 4: Create .dockerignore (AC: #7) - Subtask 4.1: Create .dockerignore file - Subtask 4.2: Add exclusions: node_modules, dist, coverage, .git - Subtask 4.3: Add additional exclusions: .env, tests, docker-compose*.yml - Subtask 4.4: Verify .dockerignore working (smaller build context)
Task 5: Test production build (AC: #5, #6) - Subtask 5.1: Build production image: `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t boilerplate:prod .` - Subtask 5.2: Verify image size: `docker images boilerplate:prod` (<200MB) - Subtask 5.3: Test container startup: `docker run -p 3000:3000 boilerplate:prod` - Subtask 5.4: Test health check: `docker exec curl -f localhost:3000/health`
Task 6: Integration testing (AC: #4, #6) - Subtask 6.1: Test with production database connection - Subtask 6.2: Verify non-root user execution - Subtask 6.3: Test environment variable loading from .env - Subtask 6.4: Verify /health endpoint returns 200 OK

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. `docker/Dockerfile` oluşturulmuş (multi-stage) 2. Stage 1: Dependencies (npm ci --only=production) 3. Stage 2: Build (npm run build, prisma generate) 4. Stage 3: Production - Base: node:20-alpine - Copy: node_modules, dist, prisma - User: node (non-root) - Expose: 3000 - CMD: node dist/main 5. Image size: < 200MB 6. Health check: Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK instruction 7. .dockerignore: node_modules, dist, coverage, .git

doc.title: Epic 11 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Story 11.1 focuses on creating a production-optimized Docker image using multi-stage build strategy to achieve minimal image size while maintaining security and performance. Acceptance criteria include multi-stage Dockerfile, Alpine base, non-root user, and image size <200MB.

doc.title: Deployment Architecture
doc.snippet: Multi-stage Dockerfile pattern with Stage 1 (deps), Stage 2 (build), Stage 3 (production). Base: node:20-alpine, USER node, EXPOSE 3000, CMD [node, dist/main].

doc.title: Deployment Architecture
doc.snippet: Required environment variables including NODE_ENV, DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, AWS credentials, and configuration validation using Joi schema.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Minimum Coverage: 70% (enforced by CI/CD). Unit tests use Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Integration tests use separate test database with cleanup between tests.

doc.title: Security Architecture
doc.snippet: Container security best practices including non-root user execution, minimal base images (Alpine), security scanning, and secrets management through environment variables.

doc.title: Story 10-5: Hot Reload Configuration
doc.snippet: Docker Compose with PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis services operational. Volume mount strategy: source code `.:/app`, node_modules excluded. Environment variables centralized in .env at project root.

doc.title: Story 10-4: Database Seed Scripts
doc.snippet: Complete seed data setup with 10 users, 3 roles, 11 permissions. Idempotency pattern using upsert. Docker Compose integration working: `docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:seed`.

doc.title: Story 10-3: Environment Variable Management
doc.snippet: All environment variables documented in .env.example. Config validation integrated in src/main.ts using Joi schema. .gitignore properly configured for security.

file.reason: Development Dockerfile pattern with node:20-alpine base, non-root user, and watch mode configuration - reference for production Dockerfile structure
Docker Compose configuration with multi-service orchestration (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis) and volume mount strategy for development environment
Build scripts: npm run build, npm run start:dev, prisma:generate, prisma:migrate:deploy - required for multi-stage build process
Application entry point (dist/main.js) - final production container command. Health check endpoint /health available for container monitoring
Database schema - prisma generate required in build stage, prisma migrate deploy used in production

story-context.constraints:
Multi-Stage Build Strategy: Separate dependency installation, build process, and runtime for optimal image size. Stage 1 (deps): npm ci --only=production. Stage 2 (build): npm run build + npx prisma generate. Stage 3 (production): Minimal runtime with Alpine base, non-root user, health check.
Image Optimization: Alpine Linux base (~5MB) reduces size. Layer caching maximizes Docker cache hits. Dev dependencies excluded from production image. Only dist/, Prisma artifacts, and production node_modules in final image.
Security Constraints: Non-root user execution (node user, UID 1000+). Minimal attack surface with Alpine base + production deps only. No build tools in runtime. Health check built-in for monitoring. No secrets hardcoded - use environment variables.
Build Process: TypeScript compilation to dist/ via npm run build. Prisma client generation required. Environment validation via Joi schema. Database migration: npx prisma migrate deploy. Health endpoint: /health available (Epic 7).
Development Environment: Docker Compose with PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis services. Volume mount: source code .:/app, node_modules excluded. Centralized .env at project root with validation. All services healthy and independent.

interface.name: Docker Multi-Stage Build
interface.signature: FROM node:20-alpine AS deps → FROM node:20-alpine AS build → FROM node:20-alpine AS production

interface.name: Build Scripts
interface.signature: npm run build (nest build), npm run start:prod (node dist/main), npx prisma generate, npx prisma migrate deploy

interface.name: Environment Configuration
interface.signature: NODE_ENV, DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, AWS credentials validated via Joi

tests.standards: Testing follows Arrange-Act-Assert pattern for unit tests. Minimum 70% coverage enforced by CI/CD. Integration tests use separate test database with cleanup. E2E tests validate complete workflows. Docker container testing includes health check validation, non-root user verification, and database connectivity. All tests must pass before merge (CI gate).

test.description: Verify docker build creates 3 stages (deps, build, production) successfully without errors
Verify Stage 1 installs only production dependencies (--only=production) and excludes dev dependencies
Verify Stage 2 runs npm run build (TypeScript compilation) and npx prisma generate successfully
Measure image size after build: docker images boilerplate:prod - should be <200MB
Verify Dockerfile contains HEALTHCHECK instruction with curl -f http://localhost:3000/health, interval 30s, timeout 10s
Run container: docker run -d boilerplate:prod, wait 30s, verify health check passes: docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}'
Run container and verify user: docker exec container whoami - should return 'node' not 'root'
Verify port 3000 exposed: docker port container - should show 3000/tcp → 0.0.0.0:3000
Test container starts and responds: docker run -d -p 3000:3000 boilerplate:prod, then curl localhost:3000/health
Verify .dockerignore excludes node_modules, dist, coverage, .git by checking build context size smaller when .dockerignore present
Run with .env file: docker run --env-file .env, verify /health/db returns connected status
Verify environment variables from .env loaded correctly in container: docker exec env | grep NODE_ENV
